description: Machine Learning is a subfield of artificial intelligence that focuses on developing algorithms and statistical models that enable computer systems to learn from data, identify patterns, and make decisions or predictions without being explicitly programmed for every task. It involves training models on large datasets to recognize complex relationships and generalize this knowledge to new, unseen data, thereby improving performance over time across various applications.

Property.name: data-driven-learning
Property.description: Machine Learning systems acquire knowledge and improve performance by processing and analyzing large datasets.

Property.name: pattern-identification
Property.description: The core function of Machine Learning is to detect and extract meaningful patterns and relationships within complex data.

Property.name: predictive-decision-making
Property.description: Machine Learning enables systems to forecast future outcomes or make autonomous decisions based on learned insights.

Property.name: algorithmic-foundation
Property.description: Machine Learning relies heavily on the design and application of various algorithms and statistical models to process data.

Property.name: performance-optimization
Property.description: Machine Learning models continuously refine their performance and accuracy through iterative training and evaluation processes.

Property.name: resource-intensive
Property.description: Machine Learning tasks, especially training large models, typically demand substantial computational resources.

Property.name: knowledge-generalization
Property.description: Machine Learning models are designed to generalize insights from training data to make accurate predictions on novel, previously unencountered data.

Property.name: ethical-considerations
Property.description: The deployment of Machine Learning systems raises important ethical questions regarding bias, privacy, and accountability.
